rationale_for_baseline_tier: A small (~1% TBSA), non-special-site superficial burn with no blisters is self-manageable with analgesia and an emollient (T0). Because burns can deepen, advise reassessment; deeper-burn or referral features route to ED/burns service (T4).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Blisters, weeping, white/leathery/charred or numb skin, a burn to the face/hands/feet/genitals/joint, a larger or circumferential burn, spreading redness, fever, or thick yellow pus
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates a deeper/high-risk burn or infection needing proper wound care/referral.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the burn blisters, weeps, turns white/leathery or numb, spreads, or you get a fever or thick yellow pus — go to the Emergency Department (or a burns service).

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear deeper-burn/infection red flags for self-monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Take regular pain relief, keep it clean with lukewarm water, apply a bland moisturiser and a loose cover for comfort. It should settle over a few days — no ice and no antibiotic creams needed.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care; ED/burns service for deeper-burn/infection features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED for blistering/deepening, spreading redness or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: ~1% TBSA superficial forearm scald; deeper-burn/referral criteria negative; simple analgesia + emollient; reassess as burns evolve.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without assessing depth/referral criteria or giving a deeper-burn safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A partial-thickness or special-site burn is treated as trivial and scars/becomes infected.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not advising reassessment as the burn evolves.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a tiny superficial burn — unnecessary.
